data.title: Talkspace misses top-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Talkspace press release (NASDAQ:TALK): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.01 in-line.
  • Revenue of $48.72M (+14.9% Y/Y) misses by $1.22M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $2.7 million, an improvement from $(0.3) million adjusted EBITDA loss in the fourth quarter of 2023.

  • Financial Guidance

    The following guidance is based on current market conditions and expectations and the information available to the Company today. For 2025, Talkspace expects:

    • Revenue to be in the range of $220 million to $235 million vs $232.79M consensus
    • Adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $14 million to $20 million

data.title: Walmart stock stumbles on soft guidance
data.description:
Walmart’s value proposition in an inflationary environment continued to resonate with an increasing number of consumers as the retail giant’s revenue increased by 4.2% in the fiscal fourth quarter of 2025, driving profits up 10%, both of which beat Wall Street’s expectations.

Shares are trading defensively, however, as the company’s outlook for the current quarter and FY26 failed to meet Wall Street’s lofty expectations.

“The slide in Walmart's share price following the release of its Q4 results could be attributable to its premium trading valuation and the lack of associated wiggle room. Nonetheless, Q4 results show that WMT is still able to demonstrate sales and earnings competency despite a challenged macroeconomic environment, one that now includes risks associated with tariffs,” Seeking Alpha analyst Justin Purohit said.

Comparable sales across all stores – excluding fuel stations – increased 4.6% from last year, with the number of transactions increasing 2.8%. Shoppers also spent more, with average ticket up another 1.8% after contracting in the prior year’s quarter. A 20% increase in online sales contributed ~290 basis points to comparable store sales. Shoppers were especially attracted to Walmart’s warehouse with Sam’s Club sales climbing 5.7% in the reported quarter, and up 7.1% excluding fuel sales, with Sam’s Club online sales continuing to gain in popularity, surging 24% from a year ago.

Accounting for the negative impact on sales from the stronger dollar, international sales were down slightly from a year ago but increased 5.7% to account for foreign exchange fluctuations.

Looking ahead to the current quarter and for the full year 2026, Walmart’s (NYSE:WMT) guidance “assumes a generally stable consumer and continued pressure from its mix of products and formats globally," the company cautioned. The expectation for Q1 sales to be up a 3% to 4% should result in a profit between $0.57 and $0.58 per share. Down 3 to 4 cents from a year ago and below the consensus estimate of $0.65.

For FY26, Walmart (NYSE:WMT) also expects sales to improve 3%-4%, translating to $698.1B at the midpoint with earnings between $2.50 to $2.60 per share. This is also less than Wall Street’s expectations for a profit of $2.76 per share on $705.14B in sales.

Shares are down 8% into Thursday's opening bell, weighing on shares of Target (TGT), Costco (COST), and Dollar General (DG)

data.title: Stock index futures slip as investors digest Walmart's earnings report
data.description:
Stock index futures slipped on Thursday as investors were left unimpressed with Walmart's (WMT) earnings report, a day after the S&P 500 notched another record high.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.51%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.26%.

On the earnings front, Walmart (WMT) shares were down 8.5% in premarket trade as its profit, and sales outlook missed estimates amid stiff competition.

Palantir (PLTR) was also down 3.9% in premarket trade as Hegseth memo called for sharp spending cut.

The initial jobless claims will report before the opening bell today, and is expected to rise to 215K.

The February Philadelphia Fed manufacturing index will also land today and is anticipated to come in at 19.4.

The January leading index, which is being released during market hours, is expected to come in at -0.1% M/M.

Wall Street's major averages seesawed on Wednesday, but the benchmark S&P 500 eventually closed at a record high as investors digested the Federal Reserve's latest minutes and President Donald Trump's latest tariff action.

"The minutes of the January Federal Reserve meeting gave a cautious policy outlook. Nervousness about the economic consequences of Trump’s policies was clear. In particular, the potential for Trump to ignite a further wave of inflation was cited," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"Markets put in a divergent performance over the last 24 hours, with U.S. assets continuing to reach fresh highs, even as the rest of the world struggled on the back of President Donald Trump’s tariff threats and concerns about Ukraine," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

data.title: Sabre reports mixed Q4 results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Sabre press release (NASDAQ:SABR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.08 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $715M (+4.1% Y/Y) misses by $1.6M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $115 million, an improvement versus Adjusted EBITDA of $96 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Free Cash Flow for the quarter was $67 million, versus Free Cash Flow of $77 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • FY25: Revenue: $3.19B consensus

data.title: Potential DOD budget cuts give Palantir 'more opportunity,' not less: Wedbush
data.description:
If the Department of Defense goes through with the type of budget cuts suggested by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's leaked memo, it would give Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) “more opportunity,” not less, Wedbush Securities argued.

Shares of Palantir slid 3.5% in premarket trading, following yesterday's 10% decline.

“The bears, which have hated Palantir from $12 to $120 in the last 18 months, now have found their latest 'silver bullet' negative thesis around PLTR being exposed to these budget cuts, and we saw a sharp sell-off in trading accordingly once this news about [the Department of Defense] hit in the afternoon,” Wedbush analysts led by Dan Ives wrote in a note to clients.

“This is exactly the opposite [of] how we believe these DOD cuts will play out as in our view Palantir's unique software approach will enable the company to gain more IT budget dollars at the Pentagon....not less, despite these initial knee-jerk reactions from the Street,” the analysts continued. “Palantir is so well positioned for this new disciplined spending environment at the Pentagon, and this will ultimately be a positive growth catalyst as the various programs are scrutinized and as Karp & Co. get a bigger seat at the table in the Beltway.”

Hegseth's memo, obtained by the Washington Post, called for 8% annual spending cuts over the next five years. However, 17 categories were said to be exempted from the cuts, including security at the southern U.S. border, the modernization of the U.S. nuclear weapon arsenal and missile defense, one-way attack drones and other areas.

Delving deeper, Wedbush analysts said that the “stepped-up” investments in artificial intelligence coming from the Trump administration, namely Project Stargate, should benefit Palantir. It is in the “the sweet spot” to benefit from federal government AI-related spending, including getting FedRAMP approval for its entire cloud services, the analysts explained.

“Palantir remains one of our top names to own in 2025, and we believe this sell-off represents another opportunity with PLTR generating traction across both federal and commercial for its entire portfolio,” the analysts, who maintained their Outperform rating and $120 price target, added.

Separately, on Thursday, Palantir was initiated with a Buy rating and $141 price target at Loop Capital.

data.title: Unity Software beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• Unity Software Inc press release (NYSE:U): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.30 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $457.1M (-25.0% Y/Y) beats by $23.63M.
  • Revenue from our strategic portfolio was $442 million, compared to $423 million, up 4% year-over-year.
  • Create Solutions revenue was $152 million, compared to $290 million in the fourth quarter 2023.
  • Grow Solutions revenue was $305 million, compared to $319 million in the fourth quarter 2023.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $106 million, with a margin of 23%.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities was $112 million.
  • Free Cash Flow was $106 million.

  • Q1 2025 Guidance1

    • Revenue of $405 to $415 million vs. consensus of $440.07M.
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $60 to $65 million.

data.title: Mister Car Wash soars after Q4 earnings show the growth story is still humming
data.description:
Mister Car Wash (NASDAQ:MCW) jumped in early trading on Thursday after the retailer topped consensus expectations on revenue growth of 9.2% and adjusted EBITDA growth of 13%.

Comparable-store sales increased 6.0% during the quarter. Mister Car Wash (NASDAQ:MCW) noted that it added approximately 14K net new UWC members in the fourth quarter and had over 2.1 million members as of December 31. The company opened 14 new greenfield locations, relocated one, and closed two locations, bringing the total net number of car wash locations operated to 514 as of the end of the quarter.

On the balance sheet, Mister Car Wash (MCW) had a cash position of $67.5 million at the end of the quarter. There were no borrowings under the company’s revolving commitment.

Looking ahead, Mister Car Wash (MCW) sees FY25 revenue of $1.038 billion to $1.064 billion (midpoint $1.051 billion consensus) vs. $1.080 billion consensus. EPS of $0.43 to $0.45 (midpoint $0.44) is seen for the full year, vs. $0.39 consensus.

Jefferies analyst David Katz said the firm continues to view MCW's long-term growth potential as attractive, and expects the company to gain share in the highly fragmented car wash industry. "A softer-than-expected outlook appears conservative and prudent," highlighted Katz.

Shares of Mister Car Wash (MCW) rallied 9.6% in premarket action to Thursday to $8.33. The 52-week high for the stock is $8.59.

data.title: Digital Bridge Q4 revenue, hit by carried interest reversal, misses consensus
data.description:
DigitalBridge Group (NYSE:DBRG) stock dipped 1.4% in Thursday premarket trading after the asset manager that invests in digital infrastructure delivered Q4 revenue, reduced by a carried interest reversal, that failed to meet Wall Street expectations.

The company expects 2025 fee-related earnings to increase 10%-20% Y/Y from 2024's $107M, implying $118M-$128M. Fee-earning equity under management is expected to rise to ~$40B at year-end from $36B at the end of 2024. Fee-related margin is expected to rise 200 basis points from last year's 32%.

Q4 after-tax distributable EPS of $0.11, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.10, rose from $0.06 in Q3 and $0.10 in Q4 2023.

Q4 revenue of $66.2M, missing the $93.9M consensus, slid from $76.1M in the prior quarter and $350.3M a year ago. The current quarter's revenue included a $46.7M carried interest reversal, compared with a reversal of $15.8M in the prior quarter and carried interest allocation of $169.7M in the year-ago period.

Fee revenue of $101.6M increased from $76.6M in Q3 and $74.0M in the year-ago Q4.

Fee-earning equity under management of $35.5B rose from $34.1B at the end of Q3 and increased 8% Y/Y, driven by organic capital formation from Co-Invest and the latest DBP flagship, offsetting a contractual "commitment to deployment" stepdown at InfraBridge.

Expenses fell to $68.2M from $76.3M in the previous quarter and $205.5M a year ago.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.

Earlier, DigitalBridge Non-GAAP EPS of $0.11, revenue of $66.17M misses by $27.77M

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: BABA, WMT, PLTR, CVNA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged down on Thursday morning as investors digested the minutes from the Federal Reserve’s latest policy meeting, which highlighted concerns that Trump’s policy proposals, particularly tariffs, could reignite inflation in the U.S.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) surged over 8% after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results, with a 6% rise in reported revenue and a 9% increase on an organic basis. The company’s 2025 guidance projects revenue between $9.9B and $10.15B, with a midpoint of $10.03B, exceeding the consensus estimate of $9.91B. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to range from $3.53B to $3.68B, while adjusted cash flow from operations is forecasted between $975M and $1.025B.
  • Herbalife (NYSE:HLF) shares jumped 18% after delivering strong Q4 results and announcing a leadership transition, with President Stephan Gratziani set to become CEO on May 1, 2025, while current CEO Michael Johnson will move to Executive Chairman. The company’s outlook projects Q1 net sales declining 1.5% to 5.5% Y/Y, though constant currency sales could be flat to up 4%, with adjusted EBITDA expected between $140M and $150M. For FY2025, net sales are forecasted to range from a 3% decline to a 3% increase, while constant currency sales could rise 1% to 7%, and adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $600M and $640M.
  • Chinese retailer Alibaba Group Holdings (NYSE:BABA) soared 10% after strong FQ3 earnings, beating expectations across key metrics. The e-commerce giant reported an adjusted net profit of 51.07B yuan ($7.01B), up 6% Y/Y, surpassing estimates. Revenue rose 8%, and adjusted EBITA grew 4% Y/Y to $7.52B. The cloud intelligence segment expanded 13.1%, while International Digital Commerce Group revenue soared 32% Y/Y, significantly outpacing expectations.

Biggest stock losers

  • Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) fell over 5% after a memo from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, obtained by the Washington Post, revealed plans for an 8% annual cut to the defense budget over the next five years. The proposed reductions, due by Feb. 24, raised concerns about potential impacts on defense contracts. However, 17 categories are reportedly exempt, including border security, nuclear weapons modernization, missile defense, and one-way attack drones.
  • Shares of Carvana (NYSE:CVNA) dropped around 10% despite a strong Q4 performance, which saw the company beat top- and bottom-line estimates, increase car sales, and improve profit per vehicle. While Carvana expects “significant growth” in retail units sold and adjusted EBITDA in FY2025, with a sequential increase in both metrics in Q1, investor concerns may have been driven by its Form S-3 filing with the SEC, allowing for the potential sale of an undisclosed number of shares. The company’s long-term targets include gross profit growth of 15%-19% and an EBITDA margin of 8%-13.5%.
  • Retail giant Walmart (NYSE:WMT) saw its shares tumble 7% as its profit and sales outlook fell short of estimates, despite exceeding Q4 expectations. For 1QFY26, the company expects net sales growth of 3.0% to 4.0% (midpoint 3.5%), below the 3.86% consensus, while adjusted EPS is forecasted between $0.57 and $0.58 (midpoint $0.575), missing the $0.65 consensus. For FY2026, Walmart projects net sales growth of 3.0% to 4.0% (midpoint 3.5%), trailing the 4.19% consensus, with adjusted EPS guidance of $2.50 to $2.60 (midpoint $2.55), compared to the $2.77 consensus. The weaker-than-expected outlook highlights ongoing competitive pressures and potential margin constraints.

data.title: Laureate Education beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Laureate Education press release (NASDAQ:LAUR): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.62 beats by $0.20.
  • Revenue of $423.4M (+3.4% Y/Y) beats by $11.9M.
  • On an organic constant currency basis1, revenue increased by 10%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $141.1 million, compared to $131.3 million for the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • New enrollments increased 5%.
  • Total enrollments increased 5%.

  • Outlook for Fiscal 2025

    Laureate's 2025 outlook shows continued growth opportunities on a constant currency basis. On an as-reported basis, based on current spot FX rates, we do expect an unfavorable translation impact from currency versus 2024 due to the weakening of the Mexican Peso in the second half of 2024.

    Based on the current foreign exchange spot rates2, Laureate currently expects its full-year 2025 results to be as follows:

    • Total enrollments expected to be in the range of 489,000 to 495,000 students, reflecting growth of 4%-5% versus 2024;
    • Revenues expected to be in the range of $1,545 million to $1,570 million vs. $1.61B consensus, reflecting flat performance to a slight decline of (1%) on an as-reported basis and growth of 6%-7% on an organic constant currency basis versus 2024, or 7%-8% growth excluding the impact from campus consolidations; and
    • Adjusted EBITDA expected to be in the range of $467 million to $477 million, reflecting growth of 4%-6% on an as-reported basis and 11%-13% on an organic constant currency basis versus 2024.
